definition: The stock-bond correlation sign during commodity shocks maps to which transmission channel dominates: (a) transitory/headline-only → negative correlation (bonds hedge equities), (b) input-cost margin compression → near-zero correlation, (c) wage-price spiral → positive correlation (both fall as Fed tightens). Template episodes: 2014-19, 2015-16 manufacturing recession, 2022 respectively.
evidence: Logical mapping consistent with KB concepts stock_bond_correlation_labor_driven and correlation_regime_shifts
2022 template confirms channel (c): Fed tightened, both stocks and bonds fell (positive correlation)
2015-16 manufacturing recession confirms channel (b): equities fell on margin compression, bonds rallied on growth fears
Pair_0 debate called this the single most valuable claim in either analysis
open_questions: Has this three-channel framework been formally tested empirically as a distinct hypothesis?
Can the dominant channel be identified in real-time before the correlation regime shift occurs?
How does the framework interact with index concentration (tech dominance in growth)?
